title: an LLM artifact store (transcripts, outputs, caches)
describe: A store of LLM artifacts: VALUABLE, irreplaceable artifacts (conversation transcripts *.jsonl, prompt/response logs, model outputs) intermixed with REGENERABLE caches (model/plugin/embedding caches). cleanup here is REPORT-ONLY: it flags valuable artifacts that have landed inside a regenerable cache directory (where a cache eviction would destroy them). backup captures only the valuable artifacts and deliberately excludes the regenerable caches — a backup of an artifact store should preserve the irreplaceable, not the disposable.

cleanup.title: LLM-artifact placement drift detector (read-only)
cleanup.note: REPORT ONLY — never moves/deletes. The shared exclude set is NOT applied here (it would skip `cache`/`plugins`, but those are exactly what we must look INSIDE to find a misplaced valuable artifact). Drift = a valuable artifact (a transcript / output by extension) whose path runs through a regenerable cache directory — it would be lost to a cache purge. Reporting it is the whole point; relocating it is the operator's call.

backup.title: self-verifying artifact backup (caches excluded)
backup.note: Excludes regenerable cache directories so the archive captures the valuable artifacts only. The exclude set is applied IDENTICALLY to the expected-file walk and to tar (the v0.2.1 invariant) so expected == archived holds.

note: FIXED scope (WORKING_METHOD §1). The valuable/regenerable split is the operator's declared policy, not model discretion.
